Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/34715/

Patch Set 2:

"Is there a test case that could be leveraged for this, that is already creating more than 65000 subdirectories? Why wasn't that test failing?"

Hmm, there are no such test for striped directory, note: 65000 subdirectory is not enough, this problem only exists if there are more than 65000 subdirectory on a single substripe. I tried this on OpenSFS cluster, and I can reproduce the problem there.

parents.subject: LU-7091 mdt: release cross-MDT lock immediately

commit.subject: LU-6984 lmv: remove nlink check in lmv_revalidate_slaves
commit.message:
LU-6984 lmv: remove nlink check in lmv_revalidate_slaves

Remove nlink < 2 check in lmv_revalidate_slaves, because
after nlink reaches to LDISKFS_LINK_MAX (65000), the inode
nlink will be set to 1.

Add test_300o in sanity to verify the case.

And also add version check for striped dir test.

Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=SLOW=yes testlist=sanity
Change-Id: I1a333ea7f68da9157679c1358df5f7a54aee8e51
Signed-off-by: di wang <di.wang@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/16490
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
